data.title: Phase IV, Effect of Rennin-Angiotensin System Blockers on Glomerular Filtration Rate in Patients With Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes With Normoalbuminuria--- A Randomized Controlled Trial
data.brief_summary: Diabetes is the leading cause of chronic kidney disease in developed countries. About 30-40% of patients with type 1 and type 2 diabetes mellitus will develop diabetic nephropathy. Microalbuminuria is often used as an early predictor of diabetic nephropathy. Many studies already demonstrated the renoprotective effect of Renin-angiotensin-system (RAS) blockers in patients with varying degree of albuminuria, few studies focus on studying the decline in glomerular filtration rate (GFR) among patien
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion criteria:

1. Age \>=35 - 80 years old who is capable to give consent
2. Chinese
3. Either (i) Type 2 diabetes mellitus (DM) diagnosed according to ADA guideline or (ii) Hypertension defined as systolic blood pressure \>140 or diastolic \>90 mmHg or taking anti-hypertensive medication to a
endpoints: change of estimated GFR calculated by MDRD equation and onset of microalbuminuria
